author: dunkin-novice
goal: Fit the daily selection board in one viewport so users don't scroll to reach Continue.
done: Scoped to @media <=640px + body.daily-shell-active: made the daily shell a 100dvh flex column and let #daily-board flex to fill the leftover height with grid-template-rows: repeat(3,1fr); cards now size by row height (height:100%, width:auto, aspect-ratio 1568/2720, max-width 64px) so the 3 rows shrink to fit on shorter phones. Grid max-height:345px keeps cards from exceeding ~64px / overflowing the column on tall screens. Tightened title/shuffle/counter/CTA top margins. Body kept scrollable (overflow visible) as a safety fallback so Continue can never be clipped.
verified: mobile-frame HTML preview (390x844 + 375x667) shows full board + Continue with no scroll
notes: Scoped to <=640px + .daily-shell-active so desktop and /today/ (no .daily-shell-active) are untouched. Could not browser-verify the real page locally; preview is an HTML/CSS mock at iPhone dimensions. On-device retest on live recommended — if cards feel too small on very short phones, cap the shrink. Trade-off: cards get smaller on short screens in exchange for no-scroll.
